_comment: Cranswick domain context — injected into Risk agent prompt. Edit here; no code change needed.

severity_rules.description: FMEA severity calibration by topic. Values 1–5. Used to set a floor or cap when the LLM scores a gap.

food_safety_haccp.note: Food safety / HACCP requirements — Severity always ≥ 4

ccp_control.note: CCP monitoring or corrective action gaps — Severity always ≥ 4

labelling_documentation.note: Labelling / documentation — Severity 2–3 depending on regulatory applicability

fmea_scoring.description: FMEA model: Risk Score = Severity × Scope × (1 / Detectability). All scales 1–5.

severity_scale.5: Regulatory breach or safety incident — consumer harm, enforcement action
severity_scale.4: Serious non-conformance — food safety risk, audit failure likely
severity_scale.3: Significant non-conformance — operational disruption, rework required
severity_scale.2: Minor non-conformance — process inefficiency, limited impact
severity_scale.1: Administrative inconvenience — no operational or safety impact

scope_scale.1: Single site, single team or shift

detectability_scale.5: Very unlikely to be caught before causing harm — no monitoring in place
detectability_scale.4: Unlikely to be caught — monitoring is informal or infrequent
detectability_scale.3: May be caught — monitoring exists but not robust
detectability_scale.2: Likely to be caught — monitoring is regular and documented
detectability_scale.1: Almost certain to be caught immediately — automated or verified control

critical.action: Auto-escalate to Standards Manager, block draft

high.action: Flag prominently in review, require SME sign-off

medium.action: Surface as amber warning in review

low.action: Log in report, note in draft

escalation_contacts.description: Who to escalate to by risk band — used in prompt context.
escalation_contacts.high: Site Technical Manager + SME sign-off required
escalation_contacts.medium: Document author to review and confirm
escalation_contacts.low: Log and monitor at next scheduled review

regulatory_references.description: Key regulatory frameworks applicable across Cranswick sites.
frameworks: Regulation (EC) 853/2004 — Specific hygiene rules for food of animal origin

glossary.description: Standard terms and abbreviations used across Cranswick. When these appear in documents without definition, agents flag them as ambiguities. Add to Definitions/Glossary or spell out on first use.

terms.definition: Serial Shipping Container Code — unique identifier for logistic units; specify how printed and where attached (front or side)
Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points
BRCGS (Brand Reputation Compliance Global Standards) — food safety certification
Control of Substances Hazardous to Health
Logistic unit for despatch; standardise with pallet/dolav/rack where context allows
Standard term for pallet, dolly, dolav, rack when used as outload equipment
Document detailing load contents; clarify if synonymous with manifest
Load documentation; driver signs after loading; clarify vs load sheet
Sheet for dolly/pallet; clarify vs dolly sheet
Pick stock in correct rotation; CMEX ensures rotation is followed
Clarify which documents; whether synonymous with manifest

standard_document_sections.description: SOP structure for Cranswick: scope → reference docs → responsibility → frequency → procedure. CleansingAgent and DraftLayoutAgent use this order.
